About

Hey, I'm Aditya Singh Bhau

Global digital transformation and service experience professional, currently at GE HealthCare. I build the AI, automation and knowledge platforms that make employee services work better for employees worldwide.

My story

My career started in 2013 on the phones at Dell, troubleshooting hardware and network issues one customer at a time. That frontline experience taught me something I've carried ever since: most service problems aren't technology problems. They're experience problems.

At HCLTech I spent seven years designing chatbot solutions integrated with enterprise HR and IT systems, learning how self-service actually gets adopted (and why it usually doesn't). I managed bot evolution cycles end to end: requirement intake, stakeholder alignment, testing, training and release coordination.

In 2020 I joined General Electric as Project Manager for HR Tools and Technologies, delivering chatbot and virtual-assistant platforms for a global workforce. When GE undertook its historic separation into GE HealthCare, GE Aerospace and GE Vernova, I helped align HR technology, service channels and knowledge management through one of the most complex corporate transformations in recent memory. Along the way, my team reduced unresolved chatbot utterances by 84% and cut annual-enrollment inquiries by 74% through smarter bot training.

Today, at GE HealthCare, I work on HR service-enablement technology across ServiceNow HRSD, HR portals, chatbots, Agentic AI and automation platforms, including Knowledge & AI content migration for a global HR transformation program.

This site is where I share what I've learned: practical, evidence-based insights on enterprise AI, HR technology and digital transformation, written for the people who have to make these things actually work.

What I believe

People first, platforms second

Technology succeeds when it removes friction from someone’s day. Adoption is the metric that matters.

Evidence over hype

AI programs should be judged by resolved queries, reduced tickets and real employee outcomes, not demos.

Playbooks scale, heroics don’t

Sustainable operations come from documentation, governance and enablement that survive team changes.

Own the outcome

From requirement intake to go-live risk tracking, the job isn’t done until it works in production, globally.

Skills & focus areas

  • Certified ScrumMaster (CSM)
  • ITIL
  • Six Sigma
  • ServiceNow Micro-Certification
